name: Funayurei
description: Funayurei are vengeful ghosts of those who died at sea, often sailors or fishermen. They appear in the waters surrounding ships, seeking to sink them and claim more souls to join their ranks. These spirits are driven by resentment and a desire for companionship in their watery graves. They are said to be able to summon storms and manipulate the ocean to achieve their goals. Sailors often took precautions to appease them, recognizing the danger they posed. Funayurei are more than just restless spirits; they actively seek retribution for their untimely deaths. Their presence is a warning to those who traverse the sea, a reminder of the respect and caution the ocean demands. They often appear as shadowy figures or glowing orbs of light, adding to their mystique and terror. The stories of Funayurei serve as cautionary tales about the perils of the sea and the importance of honoring the dead. They are a significant part of Japanese maritime folklore, embodying the fears and superstitions of seafarers.
subtitle: Vengeful spirits of the sea
appearance: Funayurei typically appear as shadowy, translucent figures, often with long, disheveled hair. They may be partially decomposed, reflecting their watery demise. Some accounts describe them as glowing orbs of light, while others depict them as skeletal figures clinging to wreckage. Their eyes are often described as empty and filled with malice. They often carry ladles or buckets to scoop water and pour it into ships.
behavior: Funayurei are driven by vengeance and a desire to drag others to their watery graves. They are known to circle ships, creating eerie noises and summoning storms. They use ladles or buckets to scoop water and pour it onto the deck, attempting to sink the vessel. They are persistent and relentless in their pursuit, targeting ships and sailors until their goals are achieved. They are generally considered malevolent spirits.

keyMyths.title: The Ladle of Doom
keyMyths.description: Funayurei use ladles to scoop water and pour it into ships, attempting to sink them. Offering the ghosts rice or sake is said to appease them, stopping their attacks.

sources.author: Shigeru Mizuki
